quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Capacitance"?

options.text: 1/f = (n-1)(1/R₁ - 1/R₂); relates focal length to the lens material (n) and surface curvatures (R₁, R₂)
C = Q/V; the ratio of charge stored to voltage applied; measured in farads (F); C = ε₀A/d for parallel plates
Zero electrical resistance below a critical temperature T_c; enables powerful electromagnets (MRI: 1.5-7T fields) and lossless power transmission
Uses r = mv/(qB) to separate ions by mass: heavier ions curve less (larger r), lighter ions curve more (smaller r)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Capacitance" is defined as: C = Q/V; the ratio of charge stored to voltage applied; measured in farads (F); C = ε₀A/d for parallel plates. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Energy stored in a capacitor"?

options.text: Dark lines on a continuous bright background; produced when cool gas absorbs specific wavelengths from white light
F = IL × B; force on a straight current-carrying wire of length L in a uniform field B
U = ½CV² = ½Q²/C = ½QV; energy is stored in the electric field between the plates
f < 0; causes parallel light to diverge as if from a virtual focal point; always produces virtual, upright, reduced images

quiz_questions.explanation: "Energy stored in a capacitor" is defined as: U = ½CV² = ½Q²/C = ½QV; energy is stored in the electric field between the plates. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Dielectric constant (κ)"?

options.text: Accelerate charged particles to high energies using electric fields and steer with magnetic fields; colliders probe fundamental particles
Energy needed to completely remove an electron: for hydrogen, E_ionization = 13.6 eV (from n=1 to n=∞)
P + ½ρv² + ρgy = constant; higher velocity means lower pressure; the basis for lift, venturi effects, and flow measurement
Insulating material between plates increases capacitance: C = κε₀A/d; κ > 1 for all materials (κ = 1 for vacuum)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Dielectric constant (κ)" is defined as: Insulating material between plates increases capacitance: C = κε₀A/d; κ > 1 for all materials (κ = 1 for vacuum). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Parallel combination"?

options.text: When a driving frequency matches an object's natural frequency, the amplitude of oscillation increases dramatically
C_total = C₁ + C₂ + ...; equivalent to a single larger capacitor; total charge = sum of individual charges
Path difference = 2nt (n = film index, t = thickness); phase shifts at boundaries with higher-n materials; produces colored patterns
Bright colored lines on a dark background; produced by excited atoms emitting photons at characteristic frequencies

quiz_questions.explanation: "Parallel combination" is defined as: C_total = C₁ + C₂ + ...; equivalent to a single larger capacitor; total charge = sum of individual charges. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Series combination"?

options.text: ΣΔV = 0 around any closed loop; based on conservation of energy; EMF gains = resistive drops
1/C_total = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂ + ...; total voltage = sum of individual voltages; same charge on each capacitor
When a driving frequency matches a natural frequency of the tube, standing waves form and the sound is amplified dramatically
F_b = ρ_fluid · V_displaced · g; an object floats when its weight equals the buoyant force (average density < fluid density)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Series combination" is defined as: 1/C_total = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂ + ...; total voltage = sum of individual voltages; same charge on each capacitor. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "C = Q/V; the ratio of charge stored to voltage applied; measured in farads (F); C = ε₀A/d for parallel plates"?

options.text: Root-mean-square speed
Wave energy
Capacitance
RMS values

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Capacitance".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "U = ½CV² = ½Q²/C = ½QV; energy is stored in the electric field between the plates"?

options.text: Malus's law
Standard Model
Rutherford model
Energy stored in a capacitor

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Energy stored in a capacitor".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Insulating material between plates increases capacitance: C = κε₀A/d; κ > 1 for all materials (κ = 1 for vacuum)"?

options.text: Speed of sound
Dielectric constant (κ)
Buoyant force (Archimedes)
Quantum superposition

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Dielectric constant (κ)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "C_total = C₁ + C₂ + ...; equivalent to a single larger capacitor; total charge = sum of individual charges"?

options.text: Ohm's law
Parallel combination
Wave energy
Entropy

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Parallel combination".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "1/C_total = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂ + ...; total voltage = sum of individual voltages; same charge on each capacitor"?

options.text: Rutherford model
Photon emission
Heat engine efficiency
Series combination

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Series combination".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Capacitors and Dielectrics is accurate?

options.text: Curie temperature: above this temperature, ferromagnets lose their permanent magnetism and become paramagnetic.
The force on a current loop creates torque: τ = nIAB sin θ, the basis for electric motors.
Converging (convex) lenses are thicker in the middle and bring parallel rays to a focus; diverging (concave) lenses are thinner in the middle and spread rays apart.
Capacitors and Dielectrics store electrical energy in an electric field.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Capacitors and Dielectrics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: EMF can be induced by changing B, changing A, or changing the angle θ between B and the area vector.
Sign conventions must be applied consistently for accurate results.
Lenses use refraction to form images.
A parallel-plate capacitor has capacitance C = ε₀A/d (area A, plate separation d).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Capacitors and Dielectrics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: The stored charge Q = CV and energy U = ½CV² = ½Q²/C = ½QV.
The electromagnetic force governs the behavior of all charged matter, from electrons in atoms to current in wires.
At the molecular level, temperature is proportional to average kinetic energy: ½m⟨v²⟩ = (3/2)kT.
Planck proposed that energy is emitted/absorbed in discrete quanta: E = nhf.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Capacitors and Dielectrics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: EMF can be induced by changing B, changing A, or changing the angle θ between B and the area vector.
Inserting a dielectric (insulating material) between the plates increases capacitance by a factor κ: C = κε₀A/d.
Heat engines convert thermal energy to work with efficiency η = W/Q_H = 1 - Q_C/Q_H.
Young's double-slit experiment demonstrates wave nature: constructive interference occurs at d sin θ = mλ (bright fringes, m = 0,±1,±2,...); destructive at d sin θ = (m+½)λ (dark fringes).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Capacitors and Dielectrics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: Beta-minus decay (β⁻): neutron → proton + electron + antineutrino (Z→Z+1).
Radioactivity and Decay describes the spontaneous transformation of unstable nuclei.
The dielectric reduces the electric field between plates and allows more charge to be stored at the same voltage.
Mass and energy are interconvertible: the mass defect in nuclear reactions is converted to kinetic energy of products and radiation.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Capacitors and Dielectrics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: Weather balloons expand as they rise because atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, and Boyle's law predicts volume increase at lower pressure
Nuclear medicine: technetium-99m (a metastable nuclear isomer) is the most widely used medical radioisotope, emitting gamma rays for diagnostic imaging of organs
Camera flashes: a capacitor charges slowly from the battery, then discharges rapidly through the flash tube—delivering high power in a short burst
Solar cells convert photon energy to electrical energy using the photoelectric effect: photons above the bandgap energy create electron-hole pairs that generate current

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Capacitors and Dielectrics.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: Photoelectric solar cells: photon energy (E = hf) must exceed the work function of the material to free electrons and generate current—the particle nature of light in action
Musical instruments: standing waves on strings and in air columns produce specific frequencies (harmonics); the length, tension, and density determine the pitch
Touchscreens (capacitive): your finger changes the local capacitance of the screen's sensor grid, detected as a touch at that location
Flash memory (USB drives, SSDs) stores data using quantum tunneling: electrons tunnel through a thin oxide layer to charge a floating gate, representing binary data

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Capacitors and Dielectrics.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Capacitors and Dielectrics applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Energy storage: supercapacitors bridge the gap between batteries and conventional capacitors, providing rapid charge/discharge for regenerative braking in electric vehicles
Noise barriers on highways: effective at blocking high-frequency (short λ) sound but less effective for low-frequency (long λ) sound that diffracts over the top
Fluorescent lighting: UV photons from mercury vapor excite phosphor coating electrons, which emit visible light as they return to lower energy levels
Velocity selectors in mass spectrometers: only ions with the correct speed enter the separation chamber, improving measurement accuracy

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Capacitors and Dielectrics.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Capacitors and Dielectrics?

options.text: Wireless charging (Qi standard): a changing magnetic field in the charging pad induces an EMF in the receiving coil inside the phone
Camera flashes: a capacitor charges slowly from the battery, then discharges rapidly through the flash tube—delivering high power in a short burst
Doppler radar: police speed guns and weather radar measure the frequency shift of reflected radio waves to determine object velocity
Radon testing: radon-222 (α emitter, t₁/₂ = 3.8 days) seeps from soil into basements; second leading cause of lung cancer after smoking

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Capacitors and Dielectrics.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Capacitors and Dielectrics in practice?

options.text: Rainbows: sunlight refracts upon entering water droplets, reflects internally, and refracts again upon exiting—dispersion separates colors at different angles
Doppler radar: police speed guns and weather radar measure the frequency shift of reflected radio waves to determine object velocity
Radon testing: radon-222 (α emitter, t₁/₂ = 3.8 days) seeps from soil into basements; second leading cause of lung cancer after smoking
Touchscreens (capacitive): your finger changes the local capacitance of the screen's sensor grid, detected as a touch at that location

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Capacitors and Dielectrics.
